qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded memory consumption via MemoryStorage without fileSize limits enables denial-of-service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory root cause is stack overflow from synchronous recursive cleanup in lib/remove-uploaded-files.js after malformed/erroring requests with many files; this finding concerns unbounded large-file buffering in MemoryStorage and missing size limits.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described uncontrolled recursion/stack overflow root cause. The only final qualifying finding is a separate denial-of-service condition based on default MemoryStorage buffering arbitrarily large uploaded files without fileSize limits.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-5528-5vmv-3xc2 describes Multer <2.1.1 denial of service via uncontrolled recursion and malformed requests, potentially causing stack overflow.

The referenced patch 7e66481f8b2e6c54b982b34c152479e096ce2752 changes lib/remove-uploaded-files.js from direct handleFile(idx + 1) recursion to setImmediate(function () { handleFile(idx + 1) }) and adds a regression test for cleaning up many files without call stack overflow.

multer-memory-exhaustion-dos/vulnerability_report.json identifies index.js defaulting to memoryStorage(), storage/memory.js buffering an entire file with concat-stream, and missing fileSize limits; it does not identify removeUploadedFiles recursive cleanup or the malformed-request stack-overflow path.
